reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade â learning can never bypass them.

last_note.summary: With only 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, dead volume, and strong ADX show confirmed positive EV and WR well above breakeven; most other buckets remain too small or mixed to confirm. UP signals, long TTC, active volume, and bullish HTF bias are failing or noisy. Sample is still too limited for robust conclusions.
warnings: Total n=53 remains very small; ignore all n<8 buckets; many high-WR buckets have borderline sample sizes and could still be noise
observe_only mode means no live validation yet
changes_since_last: No prior analysis provided; first pass on 53 trades
focus_next: Accumulate samples specifically in DOWN_STRONG + trending + normal BB + 120-240s TTC intersection
Track whether bearish MTF alignment and sell-pressure CVD continue to add edge
Monitor calibration of avg_ev_after_cost vs realized pnl as n grows

history.summary: With n=53 total settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals (n=26, wr=0.692, +15.5 pnl) and several regime buckets (trending, normal BB, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, 120-240s TTC) show confirmed positive EV after costs and win-rates above breakeven with n>=8; UP signals and long-TTC buckets remain negative. Overall edge is modest but directionally consistent with signal learning favoring DOWN. Sample remains too small for robust conclusions.

With n=53 total settled trades, overall WR=0.604 and avg_ev_after_cost=0.116 indicate modest positive expectancy concentrated in a few high-n buckets; DOWN_STRONG, trending/hurst, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong_trend ADX, dead volume, sell_pressure CVD and bearish_aligned MTF all show WR confidently above breakeven with positive EV and n>=12. Most other buckets have either n<8 or mixed/negative realized PnL despite positive EV, suggesting noise or overfit. No prior analysis provided so no changes tracked yet.

With 53 settled trades, the bot shows modest positive EV (overall wr 0.60, +1.35 USD) driven by a few robust buckets with n>=8; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, bearish MTF alignment, and sell-pressure CVD stand out as confirmed profitable while most other slices remain too small or mixed. UP signals and extreme z-score/expansion regimes continue to drag. Sample growth since last analysis is the main change, with no material degradation in the core edges.
changes_since_last: Total settled sample grew from prior to 53
bearish_aligned and normal BB buckets now meet n>=8 threshold with strong stats

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, overall WR 60.4% and positive avg EV after cost (0.116) confirm mild edge, driven by DOWN_STRONG (n=26, WR=0.692), trending Hurst (n=50, WR=0.64), normal BB state (n=16, WR=0.875), bearish_aligned MTF (n=12, WR=0.833), sell_pressure CVD (n=19, WR=0.737) and 120-240s TTC (n=26, WR=0.731). Most other buckets remain below reliable thresholds or show mixed results after costs. Sample still too small for strong confirmation outside a few high-n regimes.
changes_since_last: First analysis; sample of 53 now available with consistent positive aggregate EV

history.summary: With n=53 settled trades, only DOWN_STRONG (n=26, WR 0.69, +15.5 pnl), trending regime (n=50), normal BB state (n=16, WR 0.875), 120-240s TTC (n=26, WR 0.73), strong_trend ADX, bearish_aligned MTF, sell_pressure CVD and dead volume buckets show confirmed positive EV above breakeven after costs; all other slices remain below n=8 or mixed/noisy.
changes_since_last: first analysis; sample grew to 53 from zero

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, bearish MTF alignment, and CVD sell pressure show confirmed positive EV (win rates 0.65-0.83, n>=12). UP signals, long holds (>=240s), and active volume buckets remain unprofitable or marginal. Sample size limits confirmation outside a few high-n buckets.

With 53 settled trades (n>=8 threshold applied), confirmed positive EV patterns center on trending Hurst regime (n=50, WR 0.64), normal BB state (n=16, WR 0.875), 120-240s TTC (n=26, WR 0.731), bearish-aligned MTF (n=12, WR 0.833), sell-pressure CVD (n=19, WR 0.737), and DOWN_STRONG signals (n=26, WR 0.692), all showing positive pnl and EV>0.10 after costs. UP direction and >=240s TTC remain marginal or negative despite positive EV in some slices. Sample remains small and observe-only, with no material change from prior zero analyses.

With only 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, dead volume, and strong ADX show confirmed positive EV and WR well above breakeven; most other buckets remain too small or mixed to confirm. UP signals, long TTC, active volume, and bullish HTF bias are failing or noisy. Sample is still too limited for robust conclusions.
changes_since_last: No prior analysis provided; first pass on 53 trades

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only â never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only â never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decides; bot executes (paper). shadow=decide+grade only (no trade), follow=engine follows direction/size subject to the deterministic floor (execution realism, risk caps, freshness). Fail-closed -> no_trade.

headlines: Ongoing BTC ETF outflows persist (daily mentions)
Michael Saylor hints at additional BTC purchases
Sen. Lummis doubles down on CLARITY Act passage
Eric Trump predicts BTC to $1M amid Trump family crypto bets
Macro risk turning for BTC vs NASDAQ (per analysts)

verifier.note: independent Claude maker-checker; can only veto/shrink a Grok-proposed paper trade, never force one; fail-open; PAPER ONLY.

last_note.summary: 133 settled trades, 63.9% win rate, +$56.81 PnL, but profit factor 1.25 and avg loss ($4.69) exceeds avg win ($3.31) indicate fragile edge. Severe side imbalance (113 down vs 20 up) and underdog price floor rejecting 217 candidates suggest directional model systematically underprices UP, creating selection bias. Only 4 context buckets fired (all tier C, 1 sample each), making dimensional analysis statistically meaningless.

knob_recommendations.reason: 217 rejections suggest floor (likely 0.30) blocks valid UP opportunities; lower cautiously to 0.25 to test if UP edge exists or model is truly miscalibrated
113 down vs 20 up trades indicates systematic overconfidence in DOWN; add small penalty to rebalance and test if UP avoidance is rational or artifact
Current tier table has 1-sample buckets; require 10+ to prevent spurious demotion and ensure statistical validity before declaring contexts avoid-worthy

new_lessons.rule: If avg_loss > avg_win despite win_rate > 0.60, edge is fragile and driven by rare large wins, not consistent mispricing
Underdog price floor rejecting >60% of execution candidates warps sample: measure UP vs DOWN rejection rates separately to detect systematic directional miscalibration
Context dimension analysis requires n>=10 per bucket; with n=1, observed win_rate=0.0 is noise, not evidence of bad regime
PF=1.25 leaves no cushion for real slippage, adverse selection, or gas; require PF>1.5 before considering any live deployment

research_loop.note: strategy-layer research meta-loop: independent model proposes exploit/avoid contexts + bounded knob nudges + lessons. Observe-only unless auto_apply (whitelisted+bounded). PAPER ONLY.

recent.rule: Edge model buckets 0.1-0.5 show empirical UP rate 20-47% vs predicted 15-45% (avg +8% error); only trust edge â¥0.5 until recalibration
Avg loss $4.68 >> avg win $3.32 despite 64% WR yields profit factor 1.28; implement strict loss caps (<$3.50) or increase min size to balance risk-reward
132 trades with 85% skew to one side is <20 effective independent bets on UP; do not claim UP edge without â¥50 UP samples at â¥55% WR
Model shows 85% down-side selection with 1.4x larger losses than winsâlikely overvaluing mean-reversion in non-reverting regimes; requires directional recalibration or regime filter
216/223 execution rejects at price floor suggests threshold miscalibrated for actual market inefficiency distribution; needs empirical tuning or removal
Edge model 0.3-0.4 bucket (n=51, largest sample) realizes 29% up vs 30-40% predictedâsystematic underperformance in mid-confidence range indicates feature drift or regime mismatch
PF=1.28 with avg_loss>avg_win fails long-term viability; require PFâ¥1.5 and avg_winâ¥avg_loss before any scale consideration
132 trades across unknown regime mix cannot validate edge robustness; need â¥500 with regime stratification before claiming statistical significance
If avg_loss > avg_win despite win_rate > 0.60, edge is fragile and driven by rare large wins, not consistent mispricing
Underdog price floor rejecting >60% of execution candidates warps sample: measure UP vs DOWN rejection rates separately to detect systematic directional miscalibration
Context dimension analysis requires n>=10 per bucket; with n=1, observed win_rate=0.0 is noise, not evidence of bad regime
PF=1.25 leaves no cushion for real slippage, adverse selection, or gas; require PF>1.5 before considering any live deployment

tradingview.stop_condition: ingest only â never trade authority

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop + a liveness watchdog (last_beat_age_s/stalled). PAPER ONLY.

arbitrage.note: arb halt only on anomaly (guaranteed_profit<=0); risk-free P&L is segregated

dependency_arbitrage.note: Lane B halt on booking anomaly only; walk-forward is observability

stop_conditions.note: quantitative kill switches checked each tick from settled ledger evidence; NOT the LLM's opinion. Halting blocks NEW entries only.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

arbitrage.note: risk-free dutch book: BUY-both (asks<$1) + SELL-both (mint $1 set, sell bids>$1); deterministic P&L; NEVER blended into directional. PAPER ONLY.

last_violations.description: 15m up-mid below nested 5m up-mid: P(up_15m) cannot be < P(up_5m) for overlapping window

dependency_arbitrage.note: LCMM nested-window scanner + optional paper execution on validated nested_implication violations.

arb_graph.note: Graph is rebuilt each tick from open windows; Grok edges are never tradable alone.

accepted_dependencies.description: Both 5m windows fully contained inside 15m window 5:30-5:45

baseline_cohort_gate.note: baseline quant path: 180-240s TTC band (scaled on 15m), high edge + strong CEX; UP blocked until promoted; green_path=15m DOWN cohort only (TV observe-only)

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective â never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

bucket_evidence.rule: blocked iff confidently_losing AND fdr_significant (Wilson upper < breakeven, PF < min, BH-FDR q=0.10)

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only â they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades â never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

down_bias_gate.note: restrict-only: harvest DOWN/TV asymmetry by blocking proven-losing UP stacks

mtf_gate.note: observe-only conflict veto: blocks 4m/5m fast-pair conflict only (loop arch default). require_confirm/side_align are opt-in restrict-only layers, not trade authority.

confidence_tier.note: At 15m TTC sweet spot, TV MTF regime adjusts min_edge/max_price only. TV trade gates remain off per operator lock.
